# Antigravity Linux Installer

> Community Linux installer/updater for Google Antigravity 2.0 and Antigravity IDE. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by Google.

## Project

- Website: https://opensnap.github.io/antigravity/
- Source: https://github.com/opensnap/antigravity
- Installer: https://opensnap.github.io/antigravity/install.sh
- Official Google download page: https://antigravity.google/download

## What This Project Does

This project provides a one-command Linux installer that resolves Google Antigravity tarball URLs from the official Google download page at install or update time. It does not mirror, modify, or redistribute Google Antigravity binaries.

The installer can install or update:

- Google Antigravity 2.0 desktop app
- Google Antigravity IDE
- Linux desktop launchers
- App icons
- CLI launchers
- Update helper commands
- Optional GNOME Files/Nautilus context-menu integration

## Install Command

```bash
INSTALLER_URL="https://opensnap.github.io/antigravity/install.sh"
curl -fsSL "$INSTALLER_URL" | sudo -E env ANTIGRAVITY_LINUX_INSTALLER_URL="$INSTALLER_URL" bash -s -- --all
```

## Manage Commands

```bash
antigravity-linux --status
sudo antigravity-linux update --all
sudo antigravity-linux --uninstall
```

## Installed Paths

- Antigravity 2.0: /opt/antigravity
- Antigravity IDE: /opt/antigravity-ide
- CLI launchers: /usr/local/bin/antigravity, /usr/local/bin/antigravity-ide
- Update helper: /usr/local/bin/antigravity-linux
- Desktop entries: /usr/share/applications/
- Icons: /usr/share/icons/hicolor/
- Nautilus extension: /usr/share/nautilus-python/extensions/open-in-antigravity-ide.py

## Supported Systems

Designed for Debian and Ubuntu systems with apt. Tested on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS. Other Linux distributions can work when the required tools are already installed.

Required tools include bash, curl, tar, python3, desktop-file-utils, and xdg-utils. Optional Nautilus integration requires python3-nautilus.

## Safety Notes

The installer uses sudo because it writes system-wide files under /opt, /usr/local/bin, /usr/share/applications, and /usr/share/icons. Review install.sh before running if desired.
